perturbation.original_text: 10. Force Majeure The force majeure hereunder shall mean the natural disaster, war, political event, and adjustment of laws, regulations and state policies. If the performance of this Agreement by one Party or the Parties according to provisions agreed hereunder is directly affected by the force majeure event, the affected Party shall immediately notify the other Party or its attorney-in-fact of the situation of the force majeure event, and shall, within fifteen (15) days, provide the detailed information of the force majeure event or the reason for non-performance or partial performance or delay of performance of this Agreement as well as valid evidence thereof (which shall be issued by the notarization authority at the place where the force majeure event occurs).
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Force Majeure The force majeure hereunder shall mean the natural disaster, war, political event, and adjustment of laws, regulations and state policies. If the performance of this Agreement by one Party or the Parties according to provisions agreed hereunder is directly affected by the force majeure event, the affected Party shall immediately notify the other Party or its attorney-in-fact of the situation of the force majeure event, and shall, within two (2) days, provide the detailed information of the force majeure event or the reason for non-performance or partial performance or delay of performance of this Agreement as well as valid evidence thereof (which shall be issued by the notarization authority at the place where the force majeure event occurs).
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the time to provide detailed information about a force majeure event to two days may be unreasonably short, especially considering the requirement to obtain valid evidence from a notarization authority at the location of the event. This could violate principles of fairness and reasonable opportunity to present a case, implicitly protected under Chinese contract law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_citation: Article 78 of the 'Contract Law of the People's Republic of China'
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 78 deals with contract modification due to unforeseen circumstances. While not directly contradicting the article, requiring notification within two days places an unreasonably short timeframe for providing detailed information and evidence. This might be deemed unfair and unconscionable, especially if the force majeure event is widespread and obtaining notarized evidence within two days is practically impossible, potentially conflicting with the general principles of fairness and good faith implied in contract interpretation.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Audit Law of the People's Republic of China May 31, 2010 --------------------- Monday >>>Welcome visitor, you're not logged in. Login Subscribe Now! Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Audit Law of the People's Republic of China [Revised] 中华人民共和国审计法 [已被修订] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Standing Committee of the National People's Congress Date issued： 08-31-1994 Effective date: 01-01-1995 Level of Authority: Laws Area of Law: Auditing Revised by： Decision of the Standing Committee of the National People's Congress on Amending the Audit Law of the People's Republic of China(2006) (Issued on 02-28-2006 Effective on 06-01-2006); Order of the President of the People's Republic of China (No. 32) The Audit Law of the People's Republic of China, as adopted at the NinthSession of the Standing Committee of the Eighth National People's Congress of the People's Republic of China on August 31, 1994, is hereby issued and shall come into force as of January 1, 1995 President of the People's Republic of China: Jiang Zemin August 31, 1994 AUDIT LAW OF THE PEOPLE'S REPUBLIC OF CHINA (Adopted at the Ninth Meeting of the Standing Committee of the Eighth National People's Congress on August 31, 1994) 中华人民共和国主席令 （第３２号） 《中华人民共和国审计法》已由中华人民共和国第八届全国人民代表大会常务委员会第九次会议于１９９４年８月３１日通过，现予公布，自１９９５年１月１日起施行。 中华人民共和国主席 江泽民 １９９４年８月３１日 中华人民共和国审计法 （１９９４年８月３１日第八届全国人民代表大会常务委员会第九次会议通过） CHAPTER I GENERAL PROVISIONS 第一章 总则 Article 1 This Law is formulated in accordance with the Constitution , with a view to strengthening the State supervision through auditing, maintaining the financial and economic order of the country, promoting the building of a clean government and ensuring the sound development of the national economy. 第一条 为了加强国家的审计监督，维护国家财政经济秩序，促进廉政建设，保障国民经济健康发展，根据 宪法 ，制定本法。 Article 2 The State shall practise a system of supervision through auditing. The State Council and the local people's governments at or above the county level shall establish audit institutions. Budgetary revenues and expenditures of various departments of the State Council, of the local people's governments at various levels and their departments, financial revenues and expenditures of State-owned monetary organizations, enterprises and institutions, as well as other budgetary revenues and expenditures and financial revenues and expenditures subject to auditing according to this Law, shall be supervised through auditing in accordance with the provisions of this Law. Audit institutions shall, according to law, supervise through auditing the authenticity, legality and beneficial results of the budgetary revenues and expenditures or financial revenues and expenditures specified in the preceding paragraph. 第二条 国家实行审计监督制度。国务院和县级以上地方人民政府设立审计机关。 国务院各部门和地方各级人民政府及其各部门的财政收支，国有的金融机构和企业事业组织的财务收支，以及其他依照本法规定应当接受审计的财政收支、财务收支，依照本法规定接受审计监督。 审计机关对前款所列财政收支或者财务收支的真实、合法和效益，依法进行审计监督。 Article 3 Audit institutions shall conduct supervision through auditing in accordance with the functions and powers, as well as procedures prescribed by law. 第三条 审计机关依照法律规定的职权和程序，进行审计监督。 Article 4 The State Council and the local people's governments at or above the county level shall annually present to the standing committees of the people's congresses at the corresponding levels the audit reports prepared by audit institutions on budget implementation and other budgetary revenues and expenditures. 第四条 国务院和县级以上地方人民政府应当每年向本级人民代表大会常务委员会提出审计机关对预算执行和其他财政收支的审计工作报告。 Article 5 Audit institutions shall independently exercise their power of supervision through auditing in accordance with the law, and be free from interference by any administrative organ, public organization or individual. 第五条 审计机关依照法律规定独立行使审计监督权，不受其他行政机关、社会团体和个人的干涉。 Article 6 Audit institutions and auditors shall, in handling audit matters, be objective and fair, practical and realistic, clean and honest, and keep secrets. 第六条 审计机关和审计人员办理审计事项，应当客观公正，实事求是，廉洁奉公，保守秘密。 CHAPTER II AU
perturbation.justification: The original contract allows 15 days to provide information regarding a force majeure event. The changed contract reduces this to 2 days. The Perturbation Explanation notes that this may violate principles of fairness, especially with the need to obtain evidence from a notarization authority. The Law Explanation states that requiring notification within two days places an unreasonably short timeframe and may be deemed unfair, especially if obtaining notarized evidence within two days is practically impossible. Since this falls under Inconsistencies - Legal Contradiction, a deadline or requirement that violates federal or state law, the modification introduces a legal contradiction. Scraped Law Snippet 1 generally refers to maintaining the financial and economic order of the country, which is indirectly undermined if parties are not given sufficient time to provide information and evidence for force majeure events.
